era_context: Late Archaic Greece; the Peisistratid tyranny continues in Athens. Sparta is consolidating power in the Peloponnese. The Persian Empire controls Asia Minor and the eastern Mediterranean. In Mesopotamia, Nebuchadnezzar II is consolidating Babylonian control over the Levant following the conquest of Jerusalem. In India, the early Vedic period is transitioning toward the Upanishadic age. In China, the Spring and Autumn period continues.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Most of the Mediterranean world (no specific events recorded)
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: This year falls in a period for which contemporary documentation is extremely sparse. No specific events are recorded in surviving sources for 596 BCE. The broader context of Nebuchadnezzar II's reign and the Peisistratid tyranny in Athens continues, but no particular developments are attested for this year. The absence of recorded events reflects the fragmentary nature of ancient historical documentation; many years, especially in the pre-Classical period, leave no trace in surviving sources. This is not to say nothing happened—only that nothing was recorded or has survived to the present.
